Migrate AI SDK 5.x to 6.0\n\n## Recommended Migration Process\n\n1. Backup your project. If you use a versioning control system, make sure all previous versions are committed.\n1. Upgrade to AI SDK 6.0.\n1. Follow the breaking changes guide below.\n1. Verify your project is working as expected.\n1. Commit your changes.\n\n## AI SDK 6.0 Package Versions\n\nYou need to update the following packages to the latest versions in your `package.json` file(s):\n\n- `ai` package: `^6.0.0`\n- `@ai-sdk/provider` package: `^3.0.0`\n- `@ai-sdk/provider-utils` package: `^4.0.0`\n- `@ai-sdk/*` packages: `^3.0.0`\n\nAn example upgrade command would be:\n\n```\npnpm install ai@latest @ai-sdk/react@latest @ai-sdk/openai@latest\n```\n\n## Codemods\n\nThe AI SDK provides Codemod transformations to help upgrade your codebase when a\nfeature is deprecated, removed, or otherwise changed.\n\nCodemods are transformations that run on your codebase automatically. They\nallow you to easily apply many changes without having to manually go through\nevery file.\n\nYou can run all v6 codemods (v5 → v6 migration) by running the following command\nfrom the root of your project:\n\n```sh\nnpx @ai-sdk/codemod v6\n```\n\n\u003cNote\u003e\n  There is also an `npx @ai-sdk/codemod upgrade` command, but it runs all\n  codemods from all versions (v4, v5, and v6). Use `v6` when upgrading from v5.\n\u003c/Note\u003e\n\nIndividual codemods can be run by specifying the name of the codemod:\n\n```sh\nnpx @ai-sdk/codemod \u003ccodemod-name\u003e \u003cpath\u003e\n```\n\nFor example, to run a specific v6 codemod:\n\n```sh\nnpx @ai-sdk/codemod v6/rename-text-embedding-to-embedding src/\n```\n\n\u003cNote\u003e\n  Codemods are intended as a tool to help you with the upgrade process. They may\n  not cover all of the changes you need to make. You may need to make additional\n  changes manually.\n\u003c/Note\u003e\n\n## Codemod Table\n\n| Codemod Name                                             | Description                                                                                        |\n| -------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| `rename-text-embedding-to-embedding`                     | Renames `textEmbeddingModel` to `embeddingModel` and `textEmbedding` to `embedding` on providers   |\n| `rename-mock-v2-to-v3`                                   | Renames V2 mock classes from `ai/test` to V3 (e.g., `MockLanguageModelV2` → `MockLanguageModelV3`) |\n| `rename-tool-call-options-to-tool-execution-options`     | Renames the `ToolCallOptions` type to `ToolExecutionOptions`                                       |\n| `rename-core-message-to-model-message`                   | Renames the `CoreMessage` type to `ModelMessage`                                                   |\n| `rename-converttocoremessages-to-converttomodelmessages` | Renames `convertToCoreMessages` function to `convertToModelMessages`                               |\n| `rename-vertex-provider-metadata-key`                    | Renames `google` to `vertex` in `providerMetadata` and `providerOptions` for Google Vertex files   |\n| `wrap-tomodeloutput-parameter`                           | Wraps `toModelOutput` parameter in object destructuring (`output` → `{ output }`)                  |\n| `add-await-converttomodelmessages`                       | Adds `await` to `convertToModelMessages` calls (now async in AI SDK 6)                             |\n\n## AI SDK Core\n\n### `Experimental_Agent` to `ToolLoopAgent` Class\n\nThe `Experimental_Agent` class has been replaced with the `ToolLoopAgent` class. Two key changes:\n\n1. The `system` parameter has been renamed to `instructions`\n2. The default `stopWhen` has changed from `stepCountIs(1)` to `stepCountIs(20)`\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 5\"\nimport { Experimental_Agent as Agent, stepCountIs } from 'ai';\n__PROVIDER_IMPORT__;\n\nconst agent = new Agent({\n  model: __MODEL__,\n  system: 'You are a helpful assistant.',\n  tools: {\n    // your tools here\n  },\n  stopWhen: stepCountIs(20), // Required for multi-step agent loops\n});\n\nconst result = await agent.generate({\n  prompt: 'What is the weather in San Francisco?',\n});\n```\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\nimport { ToolLoopAgent } from 'ai';\n__PROVIDER_IMPORT__;\n\nconst agent = new ToolLoopAgent({\n  model: __MODEL__,\n  instructions: 'You are a helpful assistant.',\n  tools: {\n    // your tools here\n  },\n  // stopWhen defaults to stepCountIs(20)\n});\n\nconst result = await agent.generate({\n  prompt: 'What is the weather in San Francisco?',\n});\n```\n\nLearn more about [building agents](/docs/agents/building-agents).\n\n### `CoreMessage` Removal\n\nThe deprecated `CoreMessage` type and related functions have been removed ([PR #10710](https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/10710)). Replace `convertToCoreMessages` with `convertToModelMessages`.\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 5\"\nimport { convertToCoreMessages, type CoreMessage } from 'ai';\n\nconst coreMessages = convertToCoreMessages(messages); // CoreMessage[]\n```\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\nimport { convertToModelMessages, type ModelMessage } from 'ai';\n\nconst modelMessages = await convertToModelMessages(messages); // ModelMessage[]\n```\n\n\u003cNote\u003e\n  Use the `rename-core-message-to-model-message` and\n  `rename-converttocoremessages-to-converttomodelmessages` codemods to\n  automatically update your codebase.\n\u003c/Note\u003e\n\n### `generateObject` and `streamObject` Deprecation\n\n`generateObject` and `streamObject` have been deprecated ([PR #10754](https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/10754)).\nThey will be removed in a future version.\nUse `generateText` and `streamText` with an `output` setting instead.\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 5\"\nimport { generateObject } from 'ai';\n__PROVIDER_IMPORT__;\nimport { z } from 'zod';\n\nconst { object } = await generateObject({\n  model: __MODEL__,\n  schema: z.object({\n    recipe: z.object({\n      name: z.string(),\n      ingredients: z.array(z.object({ name: z.string(), amount: z.string() })),\n      steps: z.array(z.string()),\n    }),\n  }),\n  prompt: 'Generate a lasagna recipe.',\n});\n```\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\nimport { generateText, Output } from 'ai';\n__PROVIDER_IMPORT__;\nimport { z } from 'zod';\n\nconst { output } = await generateText({\n  model: __MODEL__,\n  output: Output.object({\n    schema: z.object({\n      recipe: z.object({\n        name: z.string(),\n        ingredients: z.array(\n          z.object({ name: z.string(), amount: z.string() }),\n        ),\n        steps: z.array(z.string()),\n      }),\n    }),\n  }),\n  prompt: 'Generate a lasagna recipe.',\n});\n```\n\nFor streaming structured data, replace `streamObject` with `streamText`:\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 5\"\nimport { streamObject } from 'ai';\n__PROVIDER_IMPORT__;\nimport { z } from 'zod';\n\nconst { partialObjectStream } = streamObject({\n  model: __MODEL__,\n  schema: z.object({\n    recipe: z.object({\n      name: z.string(),\n      ingredients: z.array(z.object({ name: z.string(), amount: z.string() })),\n      steps: z.array(z.string()),\n    }),\n  }),\n  prompt: 'Generate a lasagna recipe.',\n});\n\nfor await (const partialObject of partialObjectStream) {\n  console.log(partialObject);\n}\n```\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\nimport { streamText, Output } from 'ai';\n__PROVIDER_IMPORT__;\nimport { z } from 'zod';\n\nconst { partialOutputStream } = streamText({\n  model: __MODEL__,\n  output: Output.object({\n    schema: z.object({\n      recipe: z.object({\n        name: z.string(),\n        ingredients: z.array(\n          z.object({ name: z.string(), amount: z.string() }),\n        ),\n        steps: z.array(z.string()),\n      }),\n    }),\n  }),\n  prompt: 'Generate a lasagna recipe.',\n});\n\nfor await (const partialObject of partialOutputStream) {\n  console.log(partialObject);\n}\n```\n\nLearn more about [generating structured data](/docs/ai-sdk-core/generating-structured-data).\n\n### async `convertToModelMessages`\n\n`convertToModelMessages()` is async in AI SDK 6 to support async `Tool.toModelOutput()`.\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 5\"\nimport { convertToModelMessages } from 'ai';\n\nconst modelMessages = convertToModelMessages(uiMessages);\n```\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\nimport { convertToModelMessages } from 'ai';\n\nconst modelMessages = await convertToModelMessages(uiMessages);\n```\n\n\u003cNote\u003e\n  Use the `add-await-converttomodelmessages` codemod to automatically update\n  your codebase.\n\u003c/Note\u003e\n\n### `Tool.toModelOutput` changes\n\n`toModelOutput()` receives a parameter object with an `output` property in AI SDK 6.\n\nIn AI SDK 5, the `output` was the arguments.\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 5\"\nimport { tool } from 'ai';\n\nconst someTool = tool({\n  // ...\n  toModelOutput: output =\u003e {\n    // ...\n  },\n});\n```\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\nimport { tool } from 'ai';\n\nconst someTool = tool({\n  // ...\n  toModelOutput: ({ output }) =\u003e {\n    // ...\n  },\n});\n```\n\n\u003cNote\u003e\n  Use the `wrap-tomodeloutput-parameter` codemod to automatically update your\n  codebase.\n\u003c/Note\u003e\n\n### `cachedInputTokens` and `reasoningTokens` in `LanguageModelUsage` Deprecation\n\n`cachedInputTokens` and `reasoningTokens` in `LanguageModelUsage` have been deprecated.\n\nYou can replace `cachedInputTokens` with `inputTokenDetails.cacheReadTokens`\nand `reasoningTokens` with `outputTokenDetails.reasoningTokens`.\n\n### `ToolCallOptions` to `ToolExecutionOptions` Rename\n\nThe `ToolCallOptions` type has been renamed to `ToolExecutionOptions`\nand is now deprecated.\n\n\u003cNote\u003e\n  Use the `rename-tool-call-options-to-tool-execution-options` codemod to\n  automatically update your codebase.\n\u003c/Note\u003e\n\n### Per-Tool Strict Mode\n\nStrict mode for tools is now controlled by setting `strict` on each tool ([PR #10817](https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/10817)). This enables fine-grained control over strict tool calls, which is important since strict mode depends on the specific tool input schema.\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 5\"\n__PROVIDER_IMPORT__;\nimport { streamText, tool } from 'ai';\nimport { z } from 'zod';\n\n// Tool strict mode was controlled by strictJsonSchema\nconst result = streamText({\n  model: __MODEL__,\n  tools: {\n    calculator: tool({\n      description: 'A simple calculator',\n      inputSchema: z.object({\n        expression: z.string(),\n      }),\n      execute: async ({ expression }) =\u003e {\n        const result = eval(expression);\n        return { result };\n      },\n    }),\n  },\n  providerOptions: {\n    openai: {\n      strictJsonSchema: true, // Applied to all tools\n    },\n  },\n});\n```\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\n__PROVIDER_IMPORT__;\nimport { streamText, tool } from 'ai';\nimport { z } from 'zod';\n\nconst result = streamText({\n  model: __MODEL__,\n  tools: {\n    calculator: tool({\n      description: 'A simple calculator',\n      inputSchema: z.object({\n        expression: z.string(),\n      }),\n      execute: async ({ expression }) =\u003e {\n        const result = eval(expression);\n        return { result };\n      },\n      strict: true, // Control strict mode per tool\n    }),\n  },\n});\n```\n\n### Flexible Tool Content\n\nAI SDK 6 introduces more flexible tool output and result content support ([PR #9605](https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/9605)), enabling richer tool interactions and better support for complex tool execution patterns.\n\n### `ToolCallRepairFunction` Signature\n\nThe `system` parameter in the `ToolCallRepairFunction` type now accepts `SystemModelMessage` in addition to `string` ([PR #10635](https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/10635)). This allows for more flexible system message configuration, including provider-specific options like caching.\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 5\"\nimport type { ToolCallRepairFunction } from 'ai';\n\nconst repairToolCall: ToolCallRepairFunction\u003cMyTools\u003e = async ({\n  system, // type: string | undefined\n  messages,\n  toolCall,\n  tools,\n  inputSchema,\n  error,\n}) =\u003e {\n  // ...\n};\n```\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\nimport type { ToolCallRepairFunction, SystemModelMessage } from 'ai';\n\nconst repairToolCall: ToolCallRepairFunction\u003cMyTools\u003e = async ({\n  system, // type: string | SystemModelMessage | undefined\n  messages,\n  toolCall,\n  tools,\n  inputSchema,\n  error,\n}) =\u003e {\n  // Handle both string and SystemModelMessage\n  const systemText = typeof system === 'string' ? system : system?.content;\n  // ...\n};\n```\n\n### Embedding Model Method Rename\n\nThe `textEmbeddingModel` and `textEmbedding` methods on providers have been renamed to `embeddingModel` and `embedding` respectively. Additionally, generics have been removed from `EmbeddingModel`, `embed`, and `embedMany` ([PR #10592](https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/10592)).\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 5\"\nimport { openai } from '@ai-sdk/openai';\nimport { embed } from 'ai';\n\n// Using the full method name\nconst model = openai.textEmbeddingModel('text-embedding-3-small');\n\n// Using the shorthand\nconst model = openai.textEmbedding('text-embedding-3-small');\n\nconst { embedding } = await embed({\n  model: openai.textEmbedding('text-embedding-3-small'),\n  value: 'sunny day at the beach',\n});\n```\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\nimport { openai } from '@ai-sdk/openai';\nimport { embed } from 'ai';\n\n// Using the full method name\nconst model = openai.embeddingModel('text-embedding-3-small');\n\n// Using the shorthand\nconst model = openai.embedding('text-embedding-3-small');\n\nconst { embedding } = await embed({\n  model: openai.embedding('text-embedding-3-small'),\n  value: 'sunny day at the beach',\n});\n```\n\n\u003cNote\u003e\n  Use the `rename-text-embedding-to-embedding` codemod to automatically update\n  your codebase.\n\u003c/Note\u003e\n\n### Warning Logger\n\nAI SDK 6 introduces a warning logger that outputs deprecation warnings and best practice recommendations ([PR #8343](https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/8343)).\n\nTo disable warning logging, set the `AI_SDK_LOG_WARNINGS` environment variable to `false`:\n\n```bash\nexport AI_SDK_LOG_WARNINGS=false\n```\n\n### Warning Type Unification\n\nSeparate warning types for each generation function have been consolidated into a single `Warning` type exported from the `ai` package ([PR #10631](https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/10631)).\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 5\"\n// Separate warning types for each generation function\nimport type {\n  CallWarning,\n  ImageModelCallWarning,\n  SpeechWarning,\n  TranscriptionWarning,\n} from 'ai';\n```\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\n// Single Warning type for all generation functions\nimport type { Warning } from 'ai';\n```\n\n### Finish reason \"unknown\" merged into \"other\"\n\nThe `unknown` finish reason has been removed. It is now returned as `other`.\n\n## AI SDK UI\n\n### Tool UI Part Helper Functions Rename\n\nThe tool UI part helper functions have been renamed to better reflect their purpose and to accommodate both static and dynamic tool parts ([PR #XXXX](https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/XXXX)).\n\n#### `isToolUIPart` → `isStaticToolUIPart`\n\nThe `isToolUIPart` function has been renamed to `isStaticToolUIPart` to clarify that it checks for static tool parts only.\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 5\"\nimport { isToolUIPart } from 'ai';\n\n// Check if a part is a tool UI part\nif (isToolUIPart(part)) {\n  console.log(part.toolName);\n}\n```\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\nimport { isStaticToolUIPart } from 'ai';\n\n// Check if a part is a static tool UI part\nif (isStaticToolUIPart(part)) {\n  console.log(part.toolName);\n}\n```\n\n#### `isToolOrDynamicToolUIPart` → `isToolUIPart`\n\nThe `isToolOrDynamicToolUIPart` function has been renamed to `isToolUIPart`. The old name is deprecated but still available.\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 5\"\nimport { isToolOrDynamicToolUIPart } from 'ai';\n\n// Check if a part is either a static or dynamic tool UI part\nif (isToolOrDynamicToolUIPart(part)) {\n  console.log('Tool part found');\n}\n```\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\nimport { isToolUIPart } from 'ai';\n\n// Check if a part is either a static or dynamic tool UI part\nif (isToolUIPart(part)) {\n  console.log('Tool part found');\n}\n```\n\n#### `getToolName` → `getStaticToolName`\n\nThe `getToolName` function has been renamed to `getStaticToolName` to clarify that it returns the tool name from static tool parts only.\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 5\"\nimport { getToolName } from 'ai';\n\n// Get the tool name from a tool part\nconst name = getToolName(toolPart);\n```\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\nimport { getStaticToolName } from 'ai';\n\n// Get the tool name from a static tool part\nconst name = getStaticToolName(toolPart);\n```\n\n#### `getToolOrDynamicToolName` → `getToolName`\n\nThe `getToolOrDynamicToolName` function has been renamed to `getToolName`. The old name is deprecated but still available.\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 5\"\nimport { getToolOrDynamicToolName } from 'ai';\n\n// Get the tool name from either a static or dynamic tool part\nconst name = getToolOrDynamicToolName(toolPart);\n```\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\nimport { getToolName } from 'ai';\n\n// Get the tool name from either a static or dynamic tool part\nconst name = getToolName(toolPart);\n```\n\n## Providers\n\n### OpenAI\n\n#### `strictJsonSchema` Defaults to True\n\nThe `strictJsonSchema` setting for JSON outputs and tool calls is enabled by default ([PR #10752](https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/10752)). This improves stability and ensures valid JSON output that matches your schema.\n\nHowever, strict mode is stricter about schema requirements. If you receive schema rejection errors, adjust your schema (for example, use `null` instead of `undefined`) or disable strict mode.\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 5\"\nimport { openai } from '@ai-sdk/openai';\nimport { generateObject } from 'ai';\nimport { z } from 'zod';\n\n// strictJsonSchema was false by default\nconst result = await generateObject({\n  model: openai('gpt-5.1'),\n  schema: z.object({\n    name: z.string(),\n  }),\n  prompt: 'Generate a person',\n});\n```\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\nimport { openai } from '@ai-sdk/openai';\nimport { generateObject } from 'ai';\nimport { z } from 'zod';\n\n// strictJsonSchema is true by default\nconst result = await generateObject({\n  model: openai('gpt-5.1'),\n  schema: z.object({\n    name: z.string(),\n  }),\n  prompt: 'Generate a person',\n});\n\n// Disable strict mode if needed\nconst resultNoStrict = await generateObject({\n  model: openai('gpt-5.1'),\n  schema: z.object({\n    name: z.string(),\n  }),\n  prompt: 'Generate a person',\n  providerOptions: {\n    openai: {\n      strictJsonSchema: false,\n    } satisfies OpenAIResponsesProviderOptions,\n  },\n});\n```\n\n#### `structuredOutputs` Option Removed from Chat Model\n\nThe `structuredOutputs` provider option has been removed from chat models ([PR #10752](https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/10752)). Use `strictJsonSchema` instead.\n\n### Azure\n\n#### Default Provider Uses Responses API\n\nThe `@ai-sdk/azure` provider now uses the Responses API by default when calling `azure()` ([PR #9868](https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/9868)). To use the previous Chat Completions API behavior, use `azure.chat()` instead.\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 5\"\nimport { azure } from '@ai-sdk/azure';\n\n// Used Chat Completions API\nconst model = azure('gpt-4o');\n```\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\nimport { azure } from '@ai-sdk/azure';\n\n// Now uses Responses API by default\nconst model = azure('gpt-4o');\n\n// Use azure.chat() for Chat Completions API\nconst chatModel = azure.chat('gpt-4o');\n\n// Use azure.responses() explicitly for Responses API\nconst responsesModel = azure.responses('gpt-4o');\n```\n\n\u003cNote\u003e\n  The Responses and Chat Completions APIs have different behavior and defaults.\n  If you depend on the Chat Completions API, switch your model instance to\n  `azure.chat()` and audit your configuration.\n\u003c/Note\u003e\n\n#### Responses API `providerMetadata` and `providerOptions` Key\n\nFor the **Responses API**, the `@ai-sdk/azure` provider now uses `azure` as the key for `providerMetadata` and `providerOptions` instead of `openai`. The `openai` key is still supported for `providerOptions` input, but resulting `providerMetadata` output now uses `azure`.\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 5\"\nimport { azure } from '@ai-sdk/azure';\nimport { generateText } from 'ai';\n\nconst result = await generateText({\n  model: azure.responses('gpt-5-mini'), // use your own deployment\n  prompt: 'Hello',\n  providerOptions: {\n    openai: {\n      // AI SDK 5: use `openai` key for Responses API options\n      reasoningSummary: 'auto',\n    },\n  },\n});\n\n// Accessed metadata via 'openai' key\nconsole.log(result.providerMetadata?.openai?.responseId);\n```\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\nimport { azure } from '@ai-sdk/azure';\nimport { generateText } from 'ai';\n\nconst result = await generateText({\n  // azure() now uses the Responses API by default\n  model: azure('gpt-5-mini'), // use your own deployment\n  prompt: 'Hello',\n  providerOptions: {\n    azure: {\n      // AI SDK 6: use `azure` key for Responses API options\n      reasoningSummary: 'auto',\n    },\n  },\n});\n\n// Access metadata via 'azure' key\nconsole.log(result.providerMetadata?.azure?.responseId);\n```\n\n### Anthropic\n\n#### Structured Outputs Mode\n\nAnthropic has [ introduced native structured outputs for Claude Sonnet 4.5 and later models ](https://www.claude.com/blog/structured-outputs-on-the-claude-developer-platform). The `@ai-sdk/anthropic` provider now includes a `structuredOutputMode` option to control how structured outputs are generated ([PR #10502](https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/10502)).\n\nThe available modes are:\n\n- `'outputFormat'`: Use Anthropic's native `output_format` parameter\n- `'jsonTool'`: Use a special JSON tool to specify the structured output format\n- `'auto'` (default): Use `'outputFormat'` when supported by the model, otherwise fall back to `'jsonTool'`\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\nimport { anthropic } from '@ai-sdk/anthropic';\nimport { generateObject } from 'ai';\nimport { z } from 'zod';\n\nconst result = await generateObject({\n  model: anthropic('claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929'),\n  schema: z.object({\n    name: z.string(),\n    age: z.number(),\n  }),\n  prompt: 'Generate a person',\n  providerOptions: {\n    anthropic: {\n      // Explicitly set the structured output mode (optional)\n      structuredOutputMode: 'outputFormat',\n    } satisfies AnthropicProviderOptions,\n  },\n});\n```\n\n### Google Vertex\n\n#### `providerMetadata` and `providerOptions` Key\n\nThe `@ai-sdk/google-vertex` provider now uses `vertex` as the key for `providerMetadata` and `providerOptions` instead of `google`. The `google` key is still supported for `providerOptions` input, but resulting `providerMetadata` output now uses `vertex`.\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 5\"\nimport { vertex } from '@ai-sdk/google-vertex';\nimport { generateText } from 'ai';\n\nconst result = await generateText({\n  model: vertex('gemini-2.5-flash'),\n  providerOptions: {\n    google: {\n      safetySettings: [\n        /* ... */\n      ],\n    }, // Used 'google' key\n  },\n  prompt: 'Hello',\n});\n\n// Accessed metadata via 'google' key\nconsole.log(result.providerMetadata?.google?.safetyRatings);\n```\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\nimport { vertex } from '@ai-sdk/google-vertex';\nimport { generateText } from 'ai';\n\nconst result = await generateText({\n  model: vertex('gemini-2.5-flash'),\n  providerOptions: {\n    vertex: {\n      safetySettings: [\n        /* ... */\n      ],\n    }, // Now uses 'vertex' key\n  },\n  prompt: 'Hello',\n});\n\n// Access metadata via 'vertex' key\nconsole.log(result.providerMetadata?.vertex?.safetyRatings);\n```\n\n\u003cNote\u003e\n  Use the `rename-vertex-provider-metadata-key` codemod to automatically update\n  your codebase.\n\u003c/Note\u003e\n\n## `ai/test`\n\n### Mock Classes\n\nV2 mock classes have been removed from the `ai/test` module. Use the new V3 mock classes instead for testing.\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 5\"\nimport {\n  MockEmbeddingModelV2,\n  MockImageModelV2,\n  MockLanguageModelV2,\n  MockProviderV2,\n  MockSpeechModelV2,\n  MockTranscriptionModelV2,\n} from 'ai/test';\n```\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\nimport {\n  MockEmbeddingModelV3,\n  MockImageModelV3,\n  MockLanguageModelV3,\n  MockProviderV3,\n  MockSpeechModelV3,\n  MockTranscriptionModelV3,\n} from 'ai/test';\n```\n\n\u003cNote\u003e\n  Use the `rename-mock-v2-to-v3` codemod to automatically update your codebase.\n\u003c/Note\u003e\n"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"1e:[\"$\",\"$L32\",null,{\"children\":[[\"$\",\"$L33\",null,{\"containerAttr\":\"data-docs-container\",\"itemAttr\":\"data-docs-heading\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"flex flex-row gap-4 w-full\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"article\",null,{\"className\":\"prose prose-vercel p-4 !max-w-none w-full md:!max-w-none md:w-[calc(100dvw-304px)] xl:w-[600px] 2xl:w-[700px]\",\"data-docs-container\":\"\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"flex items-center justify-between pb-6\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"flex-1\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L34\",null,{\"breadcrumbs\":[{\"title\":\"Migration Guides\",\"href\":\"docs/migration-guides\"},{\"title\":\"Migrate AI SDK 5.x to 6.0\",\"href\":\"docs/migration-guides/migration-guide-6-0\"}]}]}],[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"shrink-0 /\u003e\"},\"data-testid\":\"geist-icon\",\"height\":16,\"strokeLinejoin\":\"round\",\"style\":{\"verticalAlign\":\"$undefined\",\"width\":20,\"height\":20,\"color\":\"currentColor\"},\"viewBox\":\"0 0 16 16\",\"width\":16}]}]]}]]}]]}]}]\n"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"3d:T418,The AI SDK provides Codemod transformations to help upgrade your codebase when a\nfeature is deprecated, removed, or otherwise changed.\n\nCodemods are transformations that run on your codebase automatically. They\nallow you to easily apply many changes without having to manually go through\nevery file.\n\nYou can run all v6 codemods (v5 → v6 migration) by running the following command\nfrom the root of your project:\n\n```sh\nnpx @ai-sdk/codemod v6\n```\n\n\u003cNote\u003e\nThere is also an `npx @ai-sdk/codemod upgrade` command, but it runs all\ncodemods from all versions (v4, v5, and v6). Use `v6` when upgrading from v5.\n\u003c/Note\u003e\n\nIndividual codemods can be run by specifying the name of the codemod:\n\n```sh\nnpx @ai-sdk/codemod \u003ccodemod-name\u003e \u003cpath\u003e\n```\n\nFor example, to run a specific v6 codemod:\n\n```sh\nnpx @ai-sdk/codemod v6/rename-text-embedding-to-embedding src/\n```\n\n\u003cNote\u003e\nCodemods are intended as a tool to help you with the upgrade process. They may\nnot cover all of the changes you need to make. You may need to make additional\nchanges manually.\n\u003c/Note\u003e3e:T657,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"| Codemod Name                                             | Description                                                                                        |\n| -------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| `rename-text-embedding-to-embedding`                     | Renames `textEmbeddingModel` to `embeddingModel` and `textEmbedding` to `embedding` on providers   |\n| `rename-mock-v2-to-v3`                                   | Renames V2 mock classes from `ai/test` to V3 (e.g., `MockLanguageModelV2` → `MockLanguageModelV3`) |\n| `rename-tool-call-options-to-tool-execution-options`     | Renames the `ToolCallOptions` type to `ToolExecutionOptions`                                       |\n| `rename-core-message-to-model-message`                   | Renames the `CoreMessage` type to `ModelMessage`                                                   |\n| `rename-converttocoremessages-to-converttomodelmessages` | Renames `convertToCoreMessages` function to `convertToModelMessages`                               |\n| `rename-vertex-provider-metadata-key`                    | Renames `google` to `vertex` in `providerMetadata` and `providerOptions` for Google Vertex files   |\n| `wrap-tomodeloutput-parameter`                           | Wraps `toModelOutput` parameter in object destructuring (`output` → `{ output }`)                  |\n| `add-await-converttomodelmessages`                       | Adds `await` to `convertToModelMessages` calls (now async in AI SDK 6)                             |"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"3f:T420,The `Experimental_Agent` class has been replaced with the `ToolLoopAgent` class. Two key changes:\n\n1. The `system` parameter has been renamed to `instructions`\n2. The default `stopWhen` has changed from `stepCountIs(1)` to `stepCountIs(20)`\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 5\"\nimport { Experimental_Agent as Agent, stepCountIs } from 'ai';\n__PROVIDER_IMPORT__;\n\nconst agent = new Agent({\nmodel: __MODEL__,\nsystem: 'You are a helpful assistant.',\ntools: {\n// your tools here\n},\nstopWhen: stepCountIs(20), // Required for multi-step agent loops\n});\n\nconst result = await agent.generate({\nprompt: 'What is the weather in San Francisco?',\n});\n```\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\nimport { ToolLoopAgent } from 'ai';\n__PROVIDER_IMPORT__;\n\nconst agent = new ToolLoopAgent({\nmodel: __MODEL__,\ninstructions: 'You are a helpful assistant.',\ntools: {\n// your tools here\n},\n// stopWhen defaults to stepCountIs(20)\n});\n\nconst result = await agent.generate({\nprompt: 'What is the weather in San Francisco?',\n});\n```\n\nLearn more about [building agents](/docs/agents/building-agents).40:T884,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"`generateObject` and `streamObject` have been deprecated ([PR #10754](https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/10754)).\nThey will be removed in a future version.\nUse `generateText` and `streamText` with an `output` setting instead.\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 5\"\nimport { generateObject } from 'ai';\n__PROVIDER_IMPORT__;\nimport { z } from 'zod';\n\nconst { object } = await generateObject({\nmodel: __MODEL__,\nschema: z.object({\nrecipe: z.object({\nname: z.string(),\ningredients: z.array(z.object({ name: z.string(), amount: z.string() })),\nsteps: z.array(z.string()),\n}),\n}),\nprompt: 'Generate a lasagna recipe.',\n});\n```\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\nimport { generateText, Output } from 'ai';\n__PROVIDER_IMPORT__;\nimport { z } from 'zod';\n\nconst { output } = await generateText({\nmodel: __MODEL__,\noutput: Output.object({\nschema: z.object({\nrecipe: z.object({\nname: z.string(),\ningredients: z.array(\nz.object({ name: z.string(), amount: z.string() }),\n),\nsteps: z.array(z.string()),\n}),\n}),\n}),\nprompt: 'Generate a lasagna recipe.',\n});\n```\n\nFor streaming structured data, replace `streamObject` with `streamText`:\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 5\"\nimport { streamObject } from 'ai';\n__PROVIDER_IMPORT__;\nimport { z } from 'zod';\n\nconst { partialObjectStream } = streamObject({\nmodel: __MODEL__,\nschema: z.object({\nrecipe: z.object({\nname: z.string(),\ningredients: z.array(z.object({ name: z.string(), amount: z.string() })),\nsteps: z.array(z.string()),\n}),\n}),\nprompt: 'Generate a lasagna recipe.',\n});\n\nfor await (const partialObject of partialObjectStream) {\nconsole.log(partialObject);\n}\n```\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\nimport { streamText, Output } from 'ai';\n__PROVIDER_IMPORT__;\nimport { z } from 'zod';\n\nconst { partialOutputStream } = streamText({\nmodel: __MODEL__,\noutput: Output.object({\nschema: z.object({\nrecipe: z.object({\nname: z.string(),\ningredients: z.array(\nz.object({ name: z.string(), amount: z.string() }),\n),\nsteps: z.array(z.string()),\n}),\n}),\n}),\nprompt: 'Generate a lasagna recipe.',\n});\n\nfor await (const partialObject of partialOutputStream) {\nconsole.log(partialObject);\n}\n```\n\nLearn more about [generating structured data](/docs/ai-sdk-core/generating-structured-data)."])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"41:T4c0,Strict mode for tools is now controlled by setting `strict` on each tool ([PR #10817](https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/10817)). This enables fine-grained control over strict tool calls, which is important since strict mode depends on the specific tool input schema.\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 5\"\n__PROVIDER_IMPORT__;\nimport { streamText, tool } from 'ai';\nimport { z } from 'zod';\n\n// Tool strict mode was controlled by strictJsonSchema\nconst result = streamText({\nmodel: __MODEL__,\ntools: {\ncalculator: tool({\ndescription: 'A simple calculator',\ninputSchema: z.object({\nexpression: z.string(),\n}),\nexecute: async ({ expression }) =\u003e {\nconst result = eval(expression);\nreturn { result };\n},\n}),\n},\nproviderOptions: {\nopenai: {\nstrictJsonSchema: true, // Applied to all tools\n},\n},\n});\n```\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\n__PROVIDER_IMPORT__;\nimport { streamText, tool } from 'ai';\nimport { z } from 'zod';\n\nconst result = streamText({\nmodel: __MODEL__,\ntools: {\ncalculator: tool({\ndescription: 'A simple calculator',\ninputSchema: z.object({\nexpression: z.string(),\n}),\nexecute: async ({ expression }) =\u003e {\nconst result = eval(expression);\nreturn { result };\n},\nstrict: true, // Control strict mode per tool\n}),\n},\n});\n```42:T4b2,The `textEmbeddingModel` and `textEmbedding` methods on providers have been renamed to `embeddingModel` and `embedding` respectively. Additionally, generics have been removed from `EmbeddingModel`, `embed`, and `embedMany` ([PR #10592](https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/10592)).\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 5\"\nimport { openai } from '@ai-sdk/openai';\nimport { embed } from 'ai';\n\n// Using the full method name\nconst model = openai.textEmbeddingModel('text-embedding-3-small');\n\n// Using the shorthand\nconst model = openai.textEmbedding('text-embedding-3-small');\n\nconst { embedding } = await embed({\nmodel: openai.textEmbedding('text-embedding-3-small'),\nvalue: 'sunny day at the beach',\n});\n```\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\nimport { openai } from '@ai-sdk/openai';\nimport { embed } from 'ai';\n\n// Using the full method name\nconst model = openai.embeddingModel('text-embedding-3-small');\n\n// Using the shorthand\nconst model = openai.embedding('text-embedding-3-small');\n\nconst { embedding } = await embed({\nmodel: openai.embedding('text-embedding-3-small'),\nvalue: 'sunny day at the beach',\n});\n```\n\n\u003cNote\u003e\nUse the `rename-text-embedding-to-embedding` codemod to automatically update\nyour codebase.\n\u003c/Note\u003e43:T52a,The `strictJsonSchema` setting for JSON outputs and tool calls is enabled by default ([PR #10752](https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/10752)). This improves stability and ensures valid JSON output that matches your schema.\n\nHowever, strict mode is stricter about schema requirements. If you receive schema rejection errors, adjust your schema (for example, use `null` instead of `undefined`) or disable strict mode.\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 5\"\nimport { openai } from '@ai-sdk/openai';\nimport { generateObject } from 'ai';\nimport { z } from 'zod';\n\n// strictJsonSchema was false by default\nconst result = await generateObject({\nmodel: openai('gpt-5.1'),\nschema: z.object({\nname: z.string(),\n}),\nprompt: 'Generate a person',\n});\n```\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\nimport { openai } from '@ai-sdk/openai';\nimport { generateObject } from 'ai';\nimport { z } from 'zod';\n\n// strictJsonSchema is true by default\nconst result = await generateObject({\nmodel: openai('gpt-5.1'),\nschema: z.object({\nname: z.string(),\n}),\nprompt: 'Generate a person',\n});\n\n// Disable strict mode if needed\nconst resultNoStrict = await generateObject({\nmodel: openai('gpt-5.1'),\nschema: z.object({\nname: z.string(),\n}),\nprompt: 'Generate a person',\nproviderOptions: {\nopenai: {\nstrictJsonSchema: false,\n} satisfies OpenAIResponsesProviderOptions,\n},\n});\n```44:T4a7,For the **Responses API**, the `@ai-sdk/azure` provider now uses `azure` as the key for `providerMetadata` and `providerOptions` instead of `openai`. The `openai` key is still supported for `providerOptions` input, but resulting `providerMetadata` output now uses `azure`.\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 5\"\nimport { azure } from '"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"@ai-sdk/azure';\nimport { generateText } from 'ai';\n\nconst result = await generateText({\nmodel: azure.responses('gpt-5-mini'), // use your own deployment\nprompt: 'Hello',\nproviderOptions: {\nopenai: {\n// AI SDK 5: use `openai` key for Responses API options\nreasoningSummary: 'auto',\n},\n},\n});\n\n// Accessed metadata via 'openai' key\nconsole.log(result.providerMetadata?.openai?.responseId);\n```\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\nimport { azure } from '@ai-sdk/azure';\nimport { generateText } from 'ai';\n\nconst result = await generateText({\n// azure() now uses the Responses API by default\nmodel: azure('gpt-5-mini'), // use your own deployment\nprompt: 'Hello',\nproviderOptions: {\nazure: {\n// AI SDK 6: use `azure` key for Responses API options\nreasoningSummary: 'auto',\n},\n},\n});\n\n// Access metadata via 'azure' key\nconsole.log(result.providerMetadata?.azure?.responseId);\n```45:T467,Anthropic has [ introduced native structured outputs for Claude Sonnet 4.5 and later models ](https://www.claude.com/blog/structured-outputs-on-the-claude-developer-platform). The `@ai-sdk/anthropic` provider now includes a `structuredOutputMode` option to control how structured outputs are generated ([PR #10502](https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/10502)).\n\nThe available modes are:\n\n- `'outputFormat'`: Use Anthropic's native `output_format` parameter\n- `'jsonTool'`: Use a special JSON tool to specify the structured output format\n- `'auto'` (default): Use `'outputFormat'` when supported by the model, otherwise fall back to `'jsonTool'`\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\nimport { anthropic } from '@ai-sdk/anthropic';\nimport { generateObject } from 'ai';\nimport { z } from 'zod';\n\nconst result = await generateObject({\nmodel: anthropic('claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929'),\nschema: z.object({\nname: z.string(),\nage: z.number(),\n}),\nprompt: 'Generate a person',\nproviderOptions: {\nanthropic: {\n// Explicitly set the structured output mode (optional)\nstructuredOutputMode: 'outputFormat',\n} satisfies AnthropicProviderOptions,\n},\n});\n```46:T484,The `@ai-sdk/google-vertex` provider now uses `vertex` as the key for `providerMetadata` and `providerOptions` instead of `google`. The `google` key is still supported for `providerOptions` input, but resulting `providerMetadata` output now uses `vertex`.\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 5\"\nimport { vertex } from '@ai-sdk/google-vertex';\nimport { generateText } from 'ai';\n\nconst result = await generateText({\nmodel: vertex('gemini-2.5-flash'),\nproviderOptions: {\ngoogle: {\nsafetySettings: [\n/* ... */\n],\n}, // Used 'google' key\n},\nprompt: 'Hello',\n});\n\n// Accessed metadata via 'google' key\nconsole.log(result.providerMetadata?.google?.safetyRatings);\n```\n\n```tsx filename=\"AI SDK 6\"\nimport { vertex } from '@ai-sdk/google-vertex';\nimport { generateText } from 'ai';\n\nconst result = await generateText({\nmodel: vertex('gemini-2.5-flash'),\nproviderOptions: {\nvertex: {\nsafetySettings: [\n/* ... */\n],\n}, // Now uses 'vertex' key\n},\nprompt: 'Hello',\n});\n\n// Access metadata via 'vertex' key\nconsole.log(result.providerMetadata?.vertex?.safetyRatings);\n```\n\n\u003cNote\u003e\nUse the `rename-vertex-provider-metadata-key` codemod to automatically update\nyour codebase.\n\u003c/Note\u003e"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"39:[\"$\",\"$L3c\",null,{\"tableOfContents\":[{\"level\":1,\"title\":\"Migrate AI SDK 5.x to 6.0\",\"slug\":\"migrate-ai-sdk-5x-to-60\",\"content\":\"\",\"children\":[{\"level\":2,\"title\":\"Recommended Migration Process\",\"slug\":\"recommended-migration-process\",\"content\":\"1. Backup your project. If you use a versioning control system, make sure all previous versions are committed.\\n1. Upgrade to AI SDK 6.0.\\n1. Follow the breaking changes guide below.\\n1. Verify your project is working as expected.\\n1. Commit your changes.\",\"children\":[]},{\"level\":2,\"title\":\"AI SDK 6.0 Package Versions\",\"slug\":\"ai-sdk-60-package-versions\",\"content\":\"You need to update the following packages to the latest versions in your `package.json` file(s):\\n\\n- `ai` package: `^6.0.0`\\n- `@ai-sdk/provider` package: `^3.0.0`\\n- `@ai-sdk/provider-utils` package: `^4.0.0`\\n- `@ai-sdk/*` packages: `^3.0.0`\\n\\nAn example upgrade command would be:\\n\\n```\\npnpm install ai@latest @ai-sdk/react@latest @ai-sdk/openai@latest\\n```\",\"children\":[]},{\"level\":2,\"title\":\"Codemods\",\"slug\":\"codemods\",\"content\":\"$3d\",\"children\":[]},{\"level\":2,\"title\":\"Codemod Table\",\"slug\":\"codemod-table\",\"content\":\"$3e\",\"children\":[]},{\"level\":2,\"title\":\"AI SDK Core\",\"slug\":\"ai-sdk-core\",\"content\":\"\",\"children\":[{\"level\":3,\"title\":\"`Experimental_Agent` to `ToolLoopAgent` Class\",\"slug\":\"experimental_agent-to-toolloopagent-class\",\"content\":\"$3f\",\"children\":[]},{\"level\":3,\"title\":\"`CoreMessage` Removal\",\"slug\":\"coremessage-removal\",\"content\":\"The deprecated `CoreMessage` type and related functions have been removed ([PR #10710](https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/10710)). Replace `convertToCoreMessages` with `convertToModelMessages`.\\n\\n```tsx filename=\\\"AI SDK 5\\\"\\nimport { convertToCoreMessages, type CoreMessage } from 'ai';\\n\\nconst coreMessages = convertToCoreMessages(messages); // CoreMessage[]\\n```\\n\\n```tsx filename=\\\"AI SDK 6\\\"\\nimport { convertToModelMessages, type ModelMessage } from 'ai';\\n\\nconst modelMessages = await convertToModelMessages(messages); // ModelMessage[]\\n```\\n\\n\u003cNote\u003e\\nUse the `rename-core-message-to-model-message` and\\n`rename-converttocoremessages-to-converttomodelmessages` codemods to\\nautomatically update your codebase.\\n\u003c/Note\u003e\",\"children\":[]},{\"level\":3,\"title\":\"`generateObject` and `streamObject` Deprecation\",\"slug\":\"generateobject-and-streamobject-deprecation\",\"content\":\"$40\",\"children\":[]},{\"level\":3,\"title\":\"async `convertToModelMessages`\",\"slug\":\"async-converttomodelmessages\",\"content\":\"`convertToModelMessages()` is async in AI SDK 6 to support async `Tool.toModelOutput()`.\\n\\n```tsx filename=\\\"AI SDK 5\\\"\\nimport { convertToModelMessages } from 'ai';\\n\\nconst modelMessages = convertToModelMessages(uiMessages);\\n```\\n\\n```tsx filename=\\\"AI SDK 6\\\"\\nimport { convertToModelMessages } from 'ai';\\n\\nconst modelMessages = await convertToModelMessages(uiMessages);\\n```\\n\\n\u003cNote\u003e\\nUse the `add-await-converttomodelmessages` codemod to automatically update\\nyour codebase.\\n\u003c/Note\u003e\",\"children\":[]},{\"level\":3,\"title\":\"`Tool.toModelOutput` changes\",\"slug\":\"tooltomodeloutput-changes\",\"content\":\"`toModelOutput()` receives a parameter object with an `output` property in AI SDK 6.\\n\\nIn AI SDK 5, the `output` was the arguments.\\n\\n```tsx filename=\\\"AI SDK 5\\\"\\nimport { tool } from 'ai';\\n\\nconst someTool = tool({\\n// ...\\ntoModelOutput: output =\u003e {\\n// ...\\n},\\n});\\n```\\n\\n```tsx filename=\\\"AI SDK 6\\\"\\nimport { tool } from 'ai';\\n\\nconst someTool = tool({\\n// ...\\ntoModelOutput: ({ output }) =\u003e {\\n// ...\\n},\\n});\\n```\\n\\n\u003cNote\u003e\\nUse the `wrap-tomodeloutput-parameter` codemod to automatically update your\\ncodebase.\\n\u003c/Note\u003e\",\"children\":[]},{\"level\":3,\"title\":\"`cachedInputTokens` and `reasoningTokens` in `LanguageModelUsage` Deprecation\",\"slug\":\"cachedinputtokens-and-reasoningtokens-in-languagemodelusage-deprecation\",\"content\":\"`cachedInputTokens` and `reasoningTokens` in `LanguageModelUsage` have been deprecated.\\n\\nYou can replace `cachedInputTokens` with `inputTokenDetails.cacheReadTokens`\\nand `reasoningTokens` with `outputTokenDetails.reasoningTokens`.\",\"children\":[]},{\"level\":3,\"title\":\"`ToolCallOptions` to `ToolExecutionOptions` Rename\",\"slug\":\"toolcalloptions-to-toolexecutionoptions-rename\",\"content\":\"The `ToolCallOptions` type has been renamed to `ToolExecutionOptions`\\nand is now deprecated.\\n\\n\u003cNote\u003e\\nUse the `rename-tool-call-options-to-tool-execution-options` codemod to\\nautomatically update your codebase.\\n\u003c/Note\u003e\",\"children\":[]},{\"level\":3,\"title\":\"Per-Tool Strict Mode\",\"slug\":\"per-tool-strict-mode\",\"content\":\"$41\",\"children\":[]},{\"level\":3,\"title\":\"Flexible Tool Content\",\"slug\":\"flexible-tool-content\",\"content\":\"AI SDK 6 introduces more flexible tool output and result content support ([PR #9605](https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/9605)), enabling richer tool interactions and better support for complex tool execution patterns.\",\"children\":[]},{\"level\":3,\"title\":\"`ToolCallRepairFunction` Signature\",\"slug\":\"toolcallrepairfunction-signature\",\"content\":\"The `system` parameter in the `ToolCallRepairFunction` type now accepts `SystemModelMessage` in addition to `string` ([PR #10635](https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/10635)). This allows for more flexible system message configuration, including provider-specific options like caching.\\n\\n```tsx filename=\\\"AI SDK 5\\\"\\nimport type { ToolCallRepairFunction } from 'ai';\\n\\nconst repairToolCall: ToolCallRepairFunction\u003cMyTools\u003e = async ({\\nsystem, // type: string | undefined\\nmessages,\\ntoolCall,\\ntools,\\ninputSchema,\\nerror,\\n}) =\u003e {\\n// ...\\n};\\n```\\n\\n```tsx filename=\\\"AI SDK 6\\\"\\nimport type { ToolCallRepairFunction, SystemModelMessage } from 'ai';\\n\\nconst repairToolCall: ToolCallRepairFunction\u003cMyTools\u003e = async ({\\nsystem, // type: string | SystemModelMessage | undefined\\nmessages,\\ntoolCall,\\ntools,\\ninputSchema,\\nerror,\\n}) =\u003e {\\n// Handle both string and SystemModelMessage\\nconst systemText = typeof system === 'string' ? system : system?.content;\\n// ...\\n};\\n```\",\"children\":[]},{\"level\":3,\"title\":\"Embedding Model Method Rename\",\"slug\":\"embedding-model-method-rename\",\"content\":\"$42\",\"children\":[]},{\"level\":3,\"title\":\"Warning Logger\",\"slug\":\"warning-logger\",\"content\":\"AI SDK 6 introduces a warning logger that outputs deprecation warnings and best practice recommendations ([PR #8343](https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/8343)).\\n\\nTo disable warning logging, set the `AI_SDK_LOG_WARNINGS` environment variable to `false`:\\n\\n```bash\\nexport AI_SDK_LOG_WARNINGS=false\\n```\",\"children\":[]},{\"level\":3,\"title\":\"Warning Type Unification\",\"slug\":\"warning-type-unification\",\"content\":\"Separate warning types for each generation function have been consolidated into a single `Warning` type exported from the `ai` package ([PR #10631](https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/10631)).\\n\\n```tsx filename=\\\"AI SDK 5\\\"\\n// Separate warning types for each generation function\\nimport type {\\nCallWarning,\\nImageModelCallWarning,\\nSpeechWarning,\\nTranscriptionWarning,\\n} from 'ai';\\n```\\n\\n```tsx filename=\\\"AI SDK 6\\\"\\n// Single Warning type for all generation functions\\nimport type { Warning } from 'ai';\\n```\",\"children\":[]},{\"level\":3,\"title\":\"Finish reason \\\"unknown\\\" merged into \\\"other\\\"\",\"slug\":\"finish-reason-unknown-merged-into-other\",\"content\":\"The `unknown` finish reason has been removed. It is now returned as `other`.\",\"children\":[]}]},{\"level\":2,\"title\":\"AI SDK UI\",\"slug\":\"ai-sdk-ui\",\"content\":\"\",\"children\":[{\"level\":3,\"title\":\"Tool UI Part Helper Functions Rename\",\"slug\":\"tool-ui-part-helper-functions-rename\",\"content\":\"The tool UI part helper functions have been renamed to better reflect their purpose and to accommodate both static and dynamic tool parts ([PR #XXXX](https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/XXXX)).\",\"children\":[{\"level\":4,\"title\":\"`isToolUIPart` → `isStaticToolUIPart`\",\"slug\":\"istooluipart--isstatictooluipart\",\"content\":\"The `isToolUIPart` function has been renamed to `isStaticToolUIPart` to clarify that it checks for static tool parts only.\\n\\n```tsx filename=\\\"AI SDK 5\\\"\\nimport { isToolUIPart } from 'ai';\\n\\n// Check if a part is a tool UI part\\nif (isToolUIPart(part)) {\\nconsole.log(part.toolName);\\n}\\n```\\n\\n```tsx filename=\\\"AI SDK 6\\\"\\nimport { isStaticToolUIPart } from 'ai';\\n\\n// Check if a part is a static tool UI part\\nif (isStaticToolUIPart(part)) {\\nconsole.log(part.toolName);\\n}\\n```\",\"children\":[]},{\"level\":4,\"title\":\"`isToolOrDynamicToolUIPart` → `isToolUIPart`\",\"slug\":\"istoolordynamictooluipart--istooluipart\",\"content\":\"The `isToolOrDynamicToolUIPart` function has been renamed to `isToolUIPart`. The old name is deprecated but still available.\\n\\n```tsx filename=\\\"AI SDK 5\\\"\\nimport { isToolOrDynamicToolUIPart } from 'ai';\\n\\n// Check if a part is either a static or dynamic tool UI part\\nif (isToolOrDynamicToolUIPart(part)) {\\nconsole.log('Tool part found');\\n}\\n```\\n\\n```tsx filename=\\\"AI SDK 6\\\"\\nimport { isToolUIPart } from 'ai';\\n\\n// Check if a part is either a static or dynamic tool UI part\\nif (isToolUIPart(part)) {\\nconsole.log('Tool part found');\\n}\\n```\",\"children\":[]},{\"level\":4,\"title\":\"`getToolName` → `getStaticToolName`\",\"slug\":\"gettoolname--getstatictoolname\",\"content\":\"The `getToolName` function has been renamed to `getStaticToolName` to clarify that it returns the tool name from static tool parts only.\\n\\n```tsx filename=\\\"AI SDK 5\\\"\\nimport { getToolName } from 'ai';\\n\\n// Get the tool name from a tool part\\nconst name = getToolName(toolPart);\\n```\\n\\n```tsx filename=\\\"AI SDK 6\\\"\\nimport { getStaticToolName } from 'ai';\\n\\n// Get the tool name from a static tool part\\nconst name = getStaticToolName(toolPart);\\n```\",\"children\":[]},{\"level\":4,\"title\":\"`getToolOrDynamicToolName` → `getToolName`\",\"slug\":\"gettoolordynamictoolname--gettoolname\",\"content\":\"The `getToolOrDynamicToolName` function has been renamed to `getToolName`. The old name is deprecated but still available.\\n\\n```tsx filename=\\\"AI SDK 5\\\"\\nimport { getToolOrDynamicToolName } from 'ai';\\n\\n// Get the tool name from either a static or dynamic tool part\\nconst name = getToolOrDynamicToolName(toolPart);\\n```\\n\\n```tsx filename=\\\"AI SDK 6\\\"\\nimport { getToolName } from 'ai';\\n\\n// Get the tool name from either a static or dynamic tool part\\nconst name = getToolName(toolPart);\\n```\",\"children\":[]}]}]},{\"level\":2,\"title\":\"Providers\",\"slug\":\"providers\",\"content\":\"\",\"children\":[{\"level\":3,\"title\":\"OpenAI\",\"slug\":\"openai\",\"content\":\"\",\"children\":[{\"level\":4,\"title\":\"`strictJsonSchema` Defaults to True\",\"slug\":\"strictjsonschema-defaults-to-true\",\"content\":\"$43\",\"children\":[]},{\"level\":4,\"title\":\"`structuredOutputs` Option Removed from Chat Model\",\"slug\":\"structuredoutputs-option-removed-from-chat-model\",\"content\":\"The `structuredOutputs` provider option has been removed from chat models ([PR #10752](https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/10752)). Use `strictJsonSchema` instead.\",\"children\":[]}]},{\"level\":3,\"title\":\"Azure\",\"slug\":\"azure\",\"content\":\"\",\"children\":[{\"level\":4,\"title\":\"Default Provider Uses Responses API\",\"slug\":\"default-provider-uses-responses-api\",\"content\":\"The `@ai-sdk/azure` provider now uses the Responses API by default when calling `azure()` ([PR #9868](https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/9868)). To use the previous Chat Completions API behavior, use `azure.chat()` instead.\\n\\n```tsx filename=\\\"AI SDK 5\\\"\\nimport { azure } from '@ai-sdk/azure';\\n\\n// Used Chat Completions API\\nconst model = azure('gpt-4o');\\n```\\n\\n```tsx filename=\\\"AI SDK 6\\\"\\nimport { azure } from '@ai-sdk/azure';\\n\\n// Now uses Responses API by default\\nconst model = azure('gpt-4o');\\n\\n// Use azure.chat() for Chat Completions API\\nconst chatModel = azure.chat('gpt-4o');\\n\\n// Use azure.responses() explicitly for Responses API\\nconst responsesModel = azure.responses('gpt-4o');\\n```\\n\\n\u003cNote\u003e\\nThe Responses and Chat Completions APIs have different behavior and defaults.\\nIf you depend on the Chat Completions API, switch your model instance to\\n`azure.chat()` and audit your configuration.\\n\u003c/Note\u003e\",\"children\":[]},{\"level\":4,\"title\":\"Responses API `providerMetadata` and `providerOptions` Key\",\"slug\":\"responses-api-providermetadata-and-provideroptions-key\",\"content\":\"$44\",\"children\":[]}]},{\"level\":3,\"title\":\"Anthropic\",\"slug\":\"anthropic\",\"content\":\"\",\"children\":[{\"level\":4,\"title\":\"Structured Outputs Mode\",\"slug\":\"structured-outputs-mode\",\"content\":\"$45\",\"children\":[]}]},{\"level\":3,\"title\":\"Google Vertex\",\"slug\":\"google-vertex\",\"content\":\"\",\"children\":[{\"level\":4,\"title\":\"`providerMetadata` and `providerOptions` Key\",\"slug\":\"providermetadata-and-provideroptions-key\",\"content\":\"$46\",\"children\":[]}]}]},{\"level\":2,\"title\":\"`ai/test`\",\"slug\":\"aitest\",\"content\":\"\",\"children\":[{\"level\":3,\"title\":\"Mock Classes\",\"slug\":\"mock-classes\",\"content\":\"V2 mock classes have been removed from the `ai/test` module. Use the new V3 mock classes instead for testing.\\n\\n```tsx filename=\\\"AI SDK 5\\\"\\nimport {\\nMockEmbeddingModelV2,\\nMockImageModelV2,\\nMockLanguageModelV2,\\nMockProviderV2,\\nMockSpeechModelV2,\\nMockTranscriptionModelV2,\\n} from 'ai/test';\\n```\\n\\n```tsx filename=\\\"AI SDK 6\\\"\\nimport {\\nMockEmbeddingModelV3,\\nMockImageModelV3,\\nMockLanguageModelV3,\\nMockProviderV3,\\nMockSpeechModelV3,\\nMockTranscriptionModelV3,\\n} from
